calendar.context: Navigation label for the 'Pro Series' page — a circuit of qualifying jiu-jitsu tournaments across Asia-Pacific

watch.context: Navigation label for watching live streams and match videos — verb, as in 'Watch matches'

subtitle.context: Tagline for the Marianas Open — a Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu championship held in Guam

asjjfBadge.context: ASJJF = Asia Sport Jiu-Jitsu Federation. Star ranking indicates tournament prestige.

tripPackages.context: Jiu-jitsu tournament prizes: winners receive funded trip packages to compete at the championship in Guam

winYourWay.context: Motivational headline for prize section — encouraging athletes to compete and win a trip to Guam

tripPackageDesc.context: Description of trip package prizes — athletes win travel to the Marianas Open International Championship

asjjfRankDynamic.context: ASJJF (Asia Sport Jiu-Jitsu Federation) star ranking badge. {{count}} is a number 1-5.

competitionMats.context: Competition mats — padded floor areas where jiu-jitsu matches take place simultaneously

qualifierTagline.context: Indicates this event is an official qualifying tournament for the Grand Championship

visaEsta.context: ESTA = Electronic System for Travel Authorization. A US visa waiver program.

visaGuamCnmi.context: CNMI = Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands. A special visa waiver program for Guam and CNMI.

visionTitle.context: BJJ = Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. This is about future live streaming features for matches.

gi.context: Gi = the traditional uniform worn in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. This is a competition category name, do NOT translate.

noGi.context: No-Gi = grappling without the traditional uniform. This is a competition category name, do NOT translate.

rooster.context: Jiu-jitsu weight class name — lightest division, typically under 57.5kg

subtitle.context: ASJJF = Asia Sport Jiu-Jitsu Federation. Ranking points are earned by competing in sanctioned events.

alone.context: In jiu-jitsu, 'Alone' means a competitor won their division bracket with no opponents — an automatic win

gi.context: Gi = the traditional uniform in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu. Competition category name, do NOT translate.

noGi.context: No-Gi = grappling without the traditional uniform. Competition category name, do NOT translate.

roadToOpen.context: The qualifying tournament series that leads to the main Marianas Open championship

title.context: Gi and no-gi are jiu-jitsu competition formats. 'Gi' is the traditional uniform; 'no-gi' is without it.
Shimizu Technology is the software company that built this website. Company name, do NOT translate.
